channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Texas lawmaker proposes bill to ban gender transition treatment for everyone, including adults
item.description: A Texas state lawmaker introduced a bill to amend the state's ban on gender transition procedures or treatment for minors to also apply to adults.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's Oval Office thrashing of Zelenskyy shows limits of Western allies' ability to sway US leader
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — All it took was 90 seconds for weeks of tortured diplomacy to unwind in spectacular fashion.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump expected to sign order designating English as the official language of the US
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order designating English as the official language of the United States, according to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy through the years: From a 'perfect' call to an Oval Office meltdown
item.description: The first time Donald Trump spoke to Volodymyr Zelenskyy, he tried to pressure Ukraine’s new leader to dig up dirt on Joe Biden ahead of the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: California lawmaker revives AI whistleblower protections concerns in new bill
item.description: The California lawmaker who introduced an AI safety protection bill later vetoed by Gov. Gavin Newsom has again introduced legislation to regulate the powerful technology. Sen.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Off the Rails at the White House
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration approves major nearly $3 billion arms sale to Israel
item.description: The Trump administration has approved a major nearly $3 billion arms sale to Israel, bypassing a normal congressional review to provide the country with more of the 2,000-pound bombs that it has used in the war against Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: European leaders pledge to stand by Ukraine after confrontational Oval Office meeting with Trump
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — European leaders pledged late Friday to stand by Ukraine in the wake of the contentious Oval Office meeting between U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What Trump's order making English the official language in the US could mean
item.description: As President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order designating English as the official language of the United States, activists and advocacy groups are alarmed by what that will mean for non-English speakers when it comes to immigration, voter access and other issues.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Europe needs to 'step up' or Zelenskyy needs to 'step down': What's next after explosive Oval Office meeting
item.description: Europe either needs to "step up" or Zelenskyy needs to "step down" after testy Oval Office meeting, according to Ukraine observers who saw the fiery exchange at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What US lawmakers are saying about the White House clash between Trump and Zelenskyy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Key Republicans and Democrats in Congress have been stalwart supporters of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, but the blowup between the Ukrainian leader and President Donald Trump is threatening to change that.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What they said: Trump, Zelenskyy and Vance's heated argument in the Oval Office
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance on Friday berated Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy over the war in Ukraine, accusing him of not showing gratitude after he challenged Vance on the question of diplomacy with Russia's Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Commissioner Ford Jr. charged with bribery, tax evasion
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- Shelby County Commissioner Edmund Ford Jr. was taken into federal custody after turning himself in Friday morning. He is charged with bribery and kickbacks concerning federal funds and six counts of attempted tax evasion, according to a federal indictment.
item.dc:creator: Katie Copeland, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump calls for ‘ceasefire now’ between Russia and Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump says he wants an “immediate” ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine and has warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to make peace or lose American support.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AG Pam Bondi demands 'full' Epstein files from FBI, vows investigation
item.description: The DOJ released hundreds of Epstein-related files Thursday to a small group of conservative influencers, but Bondi says there's still more to be uncovered.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: A movement is calling for an ‘economic blackout’ on Friday. Here’s what to know
item.description: The People's Union USA, a grassroots group, is calling on Americans to join a national boycott by not shopping for 24 hours on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: 1 thing that upended years of U.S. foreign policy
item.description: Three years after Russia launched its invasion of Ukraine, the U.S., in a split with its European allies over the war, sided with Russia at the U.N.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Florida ups the stakes for crimes by immigrants in the US illegally
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — For most people in Florida, misdemeanor theft can result in up to a year in jail and a $1,000 fine. For an immigrant illegally in Florida, that same crime now carries a felony punishment of up to five years behind bars and a $5,000 fine.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy leaves White House without signing minerals deal after Oval Office blowup
item.description: President Donald Trump berated Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for being “disrespectful” in an extraordinary Oval Office meeting, then abruptly called off the signing of a minerals deal that Trump said would have moved that country closer to ending its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nevada film tax bill would bring jobs, but questions remain as lawmakers examine details
item.description: Lawmakers dug into details of the film tax credit bill on Thursday, with film studio executives from Warner Bros. Discovery and Sony answering questions in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Social Security Administration could cut up to 50% of its workforce
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration is preparing to lay off at least 7,000 people from its workforce of 60,000, according to a person familiar with the agency’s plans who is not authorized to speak publicly.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No new bombshells in Justice Department’s release of Jeffrey Epstein files
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Attorney General Pam Bondi on Thursday released government documents related to wealthy financier Jeffrey Epstein, but the first wave of files posted on the Justice Department website have largely been circulating in the public domain for years and didn't include any new bombshells
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada's Cortez Masto slams Trump administration cuts amid bird flu response
item.description: Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto criticized the Trump administration Thursday, saying recent terminations are worsening the federal government’s response to the bird flu outbreak and the rise price of eggs.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate Republicans seek major changes to House-passed Trump budget bill
item.description: Senate Republicans are staring down a major fight to overhaul the House's budget resolution as lawmakers eye big changes. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.), with the help of President Trump’s muscle, was able to get the House's plan to enact Trump's sweeping legislative agenda past a key hurdle Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, UK's Keir Starmer speak after meetings
item.description: Starmer is expected to try to convince Trump that Ukraine and European leaders need a place at the table for peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Tampa Bay families travel to Capitol Hill to urge lawmakers for more pediatric cancer funding
item.description: Embracing photos and wearing the names of their children who were taken too soon, families took off from Tampa International Airport Wednesday morning, headed for D.C. to urge lawmakers to pass more funding for pediatric cancer research.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC
